sources.title: Wearable AI Market Size And Share | Industry Report, 2033
claims_supported: Global wearable AI market estimated at USD 43.64 billion in 2025
Market projected to reach USD 310.56 billion by 2033
CAGR of 27.83% from 2026 to 2033
2024 market size: USD 39.7 billion

sources.title: Oura reaches $11 billion valuation with new $900 million fundraise

Valuation reached $11 billion — more than double December 2024 valuation of $5.2B
Oura became Europe's first health-tracking decacorn

claims_supported: Oura's $900M Series E, October 2025
ICONIQ, Whale Rock, Atreides as co-investors

sources.title: Global Smart Glasses Shipments Soared 110% YoY in H1 2025, With Meta Capturing Over 70% Share
claims_supported: Global smart glasses shipments +110% YoY in H1 2025

sources.title: FDA announces sweeping changes to oversight of wearables, AI-enabled devices
claims_supported: FDA published guidance January 6, 2026 reducing oversight of AI-enabled software and wearables
Non-medical-grade wearables providing general health information can proceed without full premarket review
Trump administration deregulation direction for digital health products

sources.title: AI Smart Glasses to Quadruple Revenue in 2026 as Apple and Samsung Prepare to Enter the Market
claims_supported: AI smart glasses revenue projected to grow from $1.2B (2025) to $5.6B (2026) — a 4x increase
Units projected to rise from 6 million (2025) to 20 million (2026)
Apple and Samsung entering the AI smart glasses market in 2026

sources.title: CES 2026 Wearables Market Overview: From One-device Solutions to Specialization
claims_supported: Smart rings took center stage at CES 2026
Market shifting from single-device solutions to specialized form factors
Smart glasses, rings, and watches launched as most advanced health and wellness devices

sources.title: Smart rings step into the spotlight at CES 2026
claims_supported: Smart rings dominated CES 2026 floor
New devices with AI-integrated health features from multiple vendors
Shift toward discreet, always-on wearable health monitoring

sources.title: FDA Limits Oversight of AI Health Software and Wearables
claims_supported: FDA guidance exempts low-risk AI wearables from premarket oversight
Medical-grade devices claiming clinical accuracy still require 510(k) clearance
January 2026 regulatory shift

claims_supported: Oura 2024 revenue $500M (doubled YoY)

Clinical expansion: Quest Diagnostics Health Panels partnership

sources.publisher: Women in AI / Frontiers in Digital Health
sources.title: Tracking the Body, Tracking the Law: How the U.S., EU, and APAC Govern Wearable AI
claims_supported: EU AI Act places biometric/health AI in high-risk category
GDPR treats health/biometric data as special category requiring explicit consent
EU Data Act (binding Sept 2025) grants users rights over wearable-generated data
European Health Data Space Regulation in force March 26, 2025

sources.title: Meta Dominates Smart Glasses Market with 73% Share in Q1 2026
claims_supported: Meta's smart glasses market share rose to 82% as of Q1 2026 (Counterpoint)
Ray-Ban Meta Smart Glasses driving strong demand

sources.title: Wearable AI Market Size To Reach $310.56Bn By 2033
claims_supported: Healthcare segment expected to grow fastest from 2026 to 2033
Smartwatch segment holds largest share (40%+)
AI-enabled medical device market: $13.7B in 2024 → $255B by 2033

sources.title: Wearable Devices Market — Vendor Insights
claims_supported: Global wearable shipments grew 5.4% in 2024 to 534.6 million units
Apple holds 23% of smartwatch market; Huawei second at 17%
Apple and Samsung command 45% of smartwatch revenue combined

sources.title: PreEvnt's isaac breath glucose monitor debuts at CES 2026
claims_supported: PreEvnt's breath-based glucose monitor entered human clinical trials in 2025
Debuted at CES 2026 as potential path to non-invasive glucose monitoring
FDA has not cleared any smartwatch or smart ring for non-invasive glucose monitoring

unverified_claims: Search volume estimate of ~90,000/mo is an aggregate estimate across related terms ('AI wearables', 'smart health wearables', 'smart ring health') — not from a single verified keyword tool result
The $322B annual global cost of burnout figure attributed to WHO 2024 requires direct WHO report verification before publication
The $6T annual global cost of workplace injuries figure is a commonly cited estimate — verify against ILO or NSC report for publication
notes: All market size figures sourced from third-party research firms (Grand View Research, IDC, Counterpoint) which use varying market definitions. Growth rates represent projected CAGR, not confirmed historical year-over-year figures. The 'growth_3m' field uses H1 2025 smart wearable shipment data (+110% YoY per Counterpoint) as the most recent high-frequency acceleration signal available — actual 3-month period data not available from public sources.
